memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir Thomas Walsh (before 1346-1397/8)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Sir Thomas Walsh (before 1346-1397/8) was an English soldier, landowner and politician.

Background and family
The Walsh (Waleys, Wallshe) family was first mentioned in connection with Wanlip in 1248. …

zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh是John Walsh和Alice de Cliff的兒子，於從1331年到1343年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾望利普。他與Katherine結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1377年被列為tax collector。他去世於1398年在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾望利普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh是John Walsh和Alice de Cliff的儿子，于从1331年到1343年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾望利普。他与Katherine结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1377年被列为tax collector。他去世于1398年在他的家乡，埋葬在英国萊希斯特斯爾望利普。

nameMeanings.name: Walsh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, Dermot, John Patrick, Kieran, Declan, Eamonn, Colm, Donal, Seamus, Siobhan, Brennan.

English and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English walshe (Old English wælisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’. This form of the name was generally more common than Welsh , especially in Ireland, where it originally denoted a Welsh-speaking immigrant, in particular any of the Welshmen who arrived in the wake of Strongbow's Anglo-Norman invasion of 1170. In Ireland it is sometimes also a translation of Breathnach ‘Briton’.
English: in Yorkshire and Lancashire the name is sometimes a shortened form of   Walshaw  .

variants.name: Walshe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eoin, Murph.
English and Irish: variant of   Walsh  .

variants.name: McBratney
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Breatnaigh ‘son of the Briton’ (referring to the Welsh of Strathclyde). Compare Walsh and Galbraith .

variants.name: Welsh
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English welshe (Old English wēalisc, wēlisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’, for someone from Wales or a speaker of the Welsh language. Compare Walsh and Wallace .
Americanized form of Ukrainian and Jewish (from Ukraine) Voloshin or its Polish variant Wołoszyn (see Woloszyn ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh was born from 1331 to 1343, in Wanlip, Leicestershire, England as the son of John Walsh and Alice de Cliff. He married Katherine. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. In 1377, his occupation is listed as tax collector. He died in 1398, in his hometown, and was buried in Wanlip, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh est né du 1331 au 1343, à Wanlip, Leicestershire, Angleterre, fils de John Walsh et Alice de Cliff. Il a épousé Katherine. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. En 1377, sa profession de son indiquée est tax collector. Il est décédé en 1398, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Wanlip, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh wurde von 1331 bis 1343 in Wanlip, Leicestershire, England, als Sohn von John Walsh und Alice de Cliff geboren. Er hat Katherine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1377 ist sein Beruf als tax collector aufgeführt. Er starb 1398 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Wanlip, Leicestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh è nato il da 1331 a 1343 nel Wanlip, Leicestershire, England come figlio di John Walsh e Alice de Cliff. Lui sposato Katherine. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1377, la sua occupazione è indicata come tax collector. Morì nel 1398, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Wanlip, Leicestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Walshはから1331年まで1343年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアワンリップでJohn WalshとAlice de Cliffの息子として生まれた。Katherineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1377年，職業はtax collectorと記録されている。彼は1398年に故郷で死去し，イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアワンリップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh(은)는 1331년 부터 1343년 까지 영국 레이스스터실르 완립에서 John Walsh(와)과 Alice de Cliff의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Katherine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1377년, 그의 직업은 tax collector이었다. 그는 1398년 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 완립에 묻혔다.

pt.from 12 December 1374 to 3 November 1375: de 12 de dezembro de 1374 a 3 de novembro de 1375
pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh nasceu de 1331 a 1343, em Wanlip, Leicestershire, Inglaterra filho de John Walsh e Alice de Cliff. Ele casou-se com Katherine. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1377, sua profissão aparece como tax collector. Ele faleceu em 1398, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Wanlip, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Walsh родился с 1331 по 1343 в местности ванлип, лисестиршир, Англия. Его родителей звали John Walsh и Alice de Cliff. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. В 1377 году он работал по профессии tax collector. Он умер в 1398 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности ванлип, лисестиршир, Англия.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1377, su ocupación registrada era tax collector. Murió en 1398, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Wanlip, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
